question_nl: List every active mesonet station with its island, elevation, and the date of its most recent reading. Flag any station that hasn't reported in the last 7 days.
draft_sql:
SELECT
  s.station_id,
  s.name,
  s.island,
  ROUND(s.elevation_m::numeric, 0) AS elev_m,
  MAX(m.timestamp) AS last_reading,
  CASE WHEN MAX(m.timestamp) < now() - interval '7 days' THEN 'STALE'
       WHEN MAX(m.timestamp) IS NULL THEN 'NEVER'
       ELSE 'OK' END AS status
FROM mesonet_stations s
LEFT JOIN mesonet_measurements m ON m.station_id = s.station_id
GROUP BY s.station_id, s.name, s.island, s.elevation_m
ORDER BY status DESC, s.island, s.station_id;
intent: Assess mesonet network health by identifying stations that have stopped reporting
scientific_context: Mesonet stations can go offline due to hardware failure, power loss, or communication issues. Detecting stale stations quickly is essential for data quality monitoring.
reasoning_sketch: LEFT JOIN stations to measurements, GROUP BY station, check MAX(timestamp) against 7-day threshold
expected_answer_shape: Table of all 103 stations with last_reading timestamp and OK/STALE/NEVER status
pitfalls: Must use LEFT JOIN to include stations with zero measurements
NULL timestamps need separate handling (NEVER vs STALE)
follow_up_questions: What caused Kipuka Nui and Palolo Mauka to go offline?
Are the NEVER stations planned installations?
assumptions: 7 days is a reasonable staleness threshold for 5-minute reporting stations
